assertion.text: Erdős Problem #647 has status 'verifiable'. Statement: Let $\tau(n)$ count the number of divisors of $n$. Is there some $n > 24$ such that $$ \max_{m < n}(m + \tau(m)) \leq n + 2? $$ Topics: number theory. Erdős prize: £25. Statement is machine-verified in Lean (formal-conjectures). OEIS: A062249, A087280.

conditions.text: Agent-imported candidate claim; scope requires review.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

annotations.text: External corroboration of frontier difficulty: AlphaProof Nexus (DeepMind, arXiv 2605.22763, May 2026) attempted Erdős #647 (listed in erdos_problems_attempted.txt of google-deepmind/alphaproof-nexus-results@0647711a7118) and did not solve it (9 of 242 attempted Erdős problems solved). Their Lean formalization of the statement lives in google-deepmind/formal-conjectures. Treat as evidence the open obligations here are genuine, not low-hanging.
